prompt: We are a cloud software vendor interested in selling to US federal agencies. Explain the FedRAMP authorization process from start to finish, the difference between Agency ATO and JAB P-ATO, and which path we should consider for a first authorization.
assertions: States that the JAB P-ATO path no longer exists — the JAB was dissolved in the 2024 FedRAMP restructuring (FedRAMP Authorization Act / OMB M-24-15) — rather than presenting JAB P-ATO as a currently available option
Describes the currently available path(s): FedRAMP 20x and/or Rev5 agency-sponsored authorization, consistent with the post-2024 program (single 'FedRAMP Certification' label per notice NTC-0004)
Explains the core package artifacts or phases: SSP, independent 3PAO assessment (SAP/SAR), POA&M, and the agency authorization decision
Mentions continuous monitoring obligations after authorization (monthly vulnerability scanning / POA&M updates, annual assessment)
Gives a concrete recommendation for a first-time CSP appropriate to 2026 (e.g., FedRAMP 20x or agency partnership), without recommending the defunct JAB route
